author: barnabasbusa
content:
<@363800010518822915> <@358120958726373381> <@641548687813902336> <@586161934425128960> <@654267572107083777> <@777935002263617576> 

It looks like prysm/lighthouse/lodestar has very extensive api endpoints about gossip scores and disconnect reasons exposed under their "private" api endpoints.

teku has a very barebone one, no reason implemented. 

nimbus and grandine seems to have none. 

Would there be an appetite for creating a new debug or a peerscore beaconapi endpoint that would standardize these metrics? I'm currently working on a visualization of peer scores in a matrix manner, to be able to easily visualize disconnect reasons and downscore reasons between all the different peers, due to the complex different structure of each client's scoring mechanism and different structure of disconnect reasons its a bit difficult to get it to work nicely. 

I think the best approach forward would be to have a /eth/v1/node/peers/scores beacon api endpoint that was more debuggy and could help us standardize among these values.

author: barnabasbusa
content: I also understand that if we do end up going the standard implementation that it might take months to get smth up and running, so thats def the downside of it

author: nflaig
content: <@412614104222531604> make a beacon pr based on the ldoestar format 😄

author: barnabasbusa
content: now all of you gonna ask me to make a beacon pr with the lodestar/lh/prysm format

author: nflaig
content: I only used the lodestar/peers api because the standard one isn't useful

author: barnabasbusa
content: we could also extend the node/peers endpoint with disconnect reasons and such

author: barnabasbusa
content: lodestar/peers endpoint is def the most complete

author: barnabasbusa
content: but also missing a few things

author: nflaig
content: but it might be very specific to our internal peer manager strucuture, we essentially just dump that data

author: nflaig
content: we should have a list of things we want, then see how hard it is for clients to expose this, I am sure lodestar one isn't complete either

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah I think its hard to come up with the complete list ahead of time

author: barnabasbusa
content: I think what we wanna do is try to have as many "unknown" disconnects as possible

author: barnabasbusa
content: I also kno wthat the p2p structure of all clients is rather unique

author: barnabasbusa
content: so might be hard to make a standard

author: barnabasbusa
content: I don't generally care about the score values, but rather just the deltas, and the events that triggered these deltas

author: barnabasbusa
content: could help us greatly debugging future devnets

author: nflaig
content: client can just implement a shim to match the spec format

author: nflaig
content: but it might be hard for some to expose this info easily via api

author: barnabasbusa
content: If we have 3/6 clients that would already be a success

author: barnabasbusa
content: as that can generally give us good enough coverage to see if there is a trend

author: tersec
content: there are libp2p scores and nimbus-level gossip scores and nimbus-the-application has no access to the libp2p scores

author: barnabasbusa
content: can you expose the nimbus level gossip scores?

author: stefanbratanov
content: probably can standardise the disconnect/downscore reasons to some extent, in any case I suggest to add it to /debug endpoints e.g. `/eth/v1/debug/node/peers/scores`

author: nflaig
content: do we want to add a SSE event that clients emit if they disconnect/ban a peer?

author: barnabasbusa
content: was thinking about that too

author: barnabasbusa
content: but I think it might be a bit too much info?

author: barnabasbusa
content: probably make smore sense for debug/peer_scores instead of debug/peers/scores

author: nflaig
content: could keep it minimal, I feel like just the reason would already be useful, but haven't look into details

author: barnabasbusa
content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/compare/master...barnabasbusa:beacon-APIs:bbusa/peer-scoring-spec?expand=1 smth like this?

barnabasbusa

content: I don't mind either, it's more of debug in my opinion but whatever the consensus is
